title: Designing a Garden
rationale: Garden-design lesson: students read a story and draw/write garden ideas. The Agenda ends with 'Finish with a tasting' but no dish is made and the tasting is eating-only, not a structured sensory assessment, so no Tasting. No hands-on cooking of any kind.

title: Tastes Around the World
rationale: Tasting tagged because the engaging activity is a structured sensory comparison: students sample foods at country stations, record sensory observations on a graphic organizer, and compare/contrast using sentence starters and the five senses — tasting taught as a skill. All food prep is done by the teacher before class, so there is no hands-on cooking; no other skill applies.

title: Ancient China Bingo
rationale: Social-studies reading plus a bingo game; the Agenda ends 'End with a tasting' (eat an ingredient), which is sampling, not a structured tasting skill, so no Tasting. No cooking occurs.

title: Food from the Eastern Hemisphere
rationale: Agenda has students prepare fried rice (stovetop stir-fry → Sautéing & stir-frying) and sesame cauliflower on a baking sheet in the oven (→ Roasting); knives/cutting boards plus cauliflower, carrots and onion mean students cut produce (→ Knife skills). Graters/measuring bowls appear only in the tool list with no student step shown in the Agenda, so grating/measuring were not tagged. Closing is eating-and-discussing, not structured tasting.

title: Fruit Salad Challenge
rationale: Teams cut fruit (knives in materials; 'cut fruit into bite size pieces') → Knife skills, and build a fruit salad with no heat technique → Assembling dishes. Tasting tagged because the closing is a structured blind taste test with a comparing-words worksheet and tasting vocabulary to justify a favorite. The honey/lemon/cinnamon dressing appears only as an optional 'tip,' not a reliable Agenda step, so Creating sauces & dressings was not tagged.

title: Go, Grow, Glow
rationale: Students prepare burrito-bowl components: cutting boards/knives plus chopping for the tomato-corn salsa → Knife skills; black beans heated on cooktops/pans → Boiling & simmering; building the burrito bowl from components → Assembling dishes. Rice is pre-cooked. No structured tasting (closing is a discussion), and no taught safety segment, so neither Tasting nor Kitchen & food safety.

title: 5th Grade Food Cultures Unit Overview
rationale: This is a six-lesson unit overview, not a single lesson; the Agenda only says generically 'Prepare recipe with students' without showing any specific technique, and the named dishes (vareniki, dumplings, plov, plantain fries) are described as pre-made in advance with staggered tastings. With no affirmative per-skill evidence in the Agenda, the precise call is empty. The 'wash hands/use hand sanitizer' line is routine handling, not a taught safety segment, so no Kitchen & food safety.

title: Chickpea Salad
rationale: Students chop celery and bell peppers (→ Knife skills), make a tahini-lemon-oil-salt dressing shaken in a jar (→ Creating sauces & dressings), and combine chopped vegetables with chickpeas and dressing into a salad (→ Assembling dishes). Ingredients are pre-measured by the teacher, so no Measuring; the closing thumbs-vote is a preference vote, not structured Tasting.

title: Pico de Gallo (Knife Skills)
rationale: Dedicated knife-skills lesson: students are taught chopping/dicing/mincing and practice cutting tomato, onion, pepper and cilantro → Knife skills. Kitchen & food safety is tagged because the Agenda delivers an explicit knife-safety segment ('how to hold a knife, what not to do, bear claw and saw') as taught content. Adding lime juice and salt makes the fresh salsa → Creating sauces & dressings. The closing tried/liked/loved-it vote is preference, not a structured sensory assessment, so no Tasting.

title: African American Foodways: Hoppin' John
rationale: Students dice bell peppers, celery and onions (→ Knife skills) and cook Hoppin' John (rice + black-eyed peas) in a large pan on the stove (→ Boiling & simmering); the cornbread table makes cornbread batter (→ Mixing & stirring) baked in the oven/baking pan (→ Baking). The closing is eating together with no structured tasting, and safe handling is incidental, so neither Tasting nor Kitchen & food safety.

title: Vegetable Ramen
rationale: Agenda prep/cook steps: boil noodles (→ Boiling & simmering); mince garlic and chop onion, mushrooms and broccoli (→ Knife skills); peel and grate ginger, carrots and cabbage (→ Grating); make furikake seasoning for the toppings bar (→ Seasoning & spice blending). Broth is pre-made. The 'use extra caution with graters' line is an anticipated-problem note, not a taught safety segment, so no Kitchen & food safety; the closing is eating, not structured tasting.

title: Healthy Snacks (Energy Bites, Popcorn, Cracker Stack)
rationale: No-cook assembly stations: students roll pre-made energy-bite dough into balls and roll in coconut, and build cracker stacks with sunbutter and apple (→ Assembling dishes); the popcorn station adds a pinch of seasonings and shakes to mix (→ Seasoning & spice blending). Apple-slicing knives are conditional ('if students will be cutting') and apples are pre-cut in prep, so Knife skills was not tagged. The reflection 'describe the taste' is discussion, not a structured tasting skill, so no Tasting.

title: 3rd Grade Chinese-Style Dumplings
rationale: Task-card-driven dumpling cook: students measure soy sauce/sesame oil/oil/vinegar/white pepper (→ Measuring), cut scallions (→ Knife skills), mash cooked soybeans with a masher (→ Mashing), grate garlic, ginger and carrot (→ Grating), take turns mixing the filling (→ Mixing & stirring), and fill, fold and pinch wonton wrappers (→ Wrapping & rolling), then dumplings are steamed (→ Steaming). Per-student task cards make recipe-following an explicit activity (→ Reading & following recipes). Wonton wrappers are store-bought so no Dough making, and the body shows steaming (no sauté) despite the header skill list. Closing is eating, not structured tasting.

title: A Visit to Mashama Bailey's Restaurant
rationale: Students add seasoning to pre-cooked grits and stir them (→ Seasoning & spice blending, Mixing & stirring) and make stewed collard greens — seasoning with salt/sugar/red-pepper-flakes/oil/vinegar and cooking on an induction burner (→ Boiling & simmering, reinforcing Seasoning & spice blending). Collards are cut with scissors only (not knives), so Knife skills was not tagged; the closing is sharing the tasting (eating), not a structured sensory assessment, so no Tasting.

title: Bees
rationale: Garden/craft lesson: students make a bee puppet, role-play pollination and plant flower seeds. The closing has students taste honey, which is sampling, not a structured tasting skill, so no Tasting. No cooking occurs.

title: Recycle Gardening
rationale: Garden lesson on urban container gardening: students plant seeds in recycled containers. No food preparation of any kind, so an empty array.

title: Seeds
rationale: Garden lesson: a 'seed dance' and seed-saving (removing seeds from pods/fruit). The Agenda ends 'Finish with a tasting,' which is eating, not a structured tasting skill. No cooking occurs.

title: edibleschoolyards_planting
rationale: Garden science lesson: students observe and compare plant structures, measure stems in centimeters, and plant in the garden. No food preparation, so an empty array (the measuring here is of plant length, not a cooking task).

title: 2nd Grade Decomposition Experiment Part 1
rationale: Science lesson: students fill ziploc bags with soil/compost and items and record predictions about decomposition. The Agenda ends 'Finish with a tasting' (eating), not a structured tasting skill. No cooking occurs.

title: Gardening & The Environment
rationale: Garden-jobs lesson (planting, weeding, harvesting, composting, watering) with a closing tasting of in-season fruit (eating, not a tasting skill). No cooking occurs.

title: The Garden in the Spring
rationale: Garden scavenger-hunt lesson: students search the garden for laminated signs of spring. No food preparation, so an empty array.

title: September Sixth Grade Summer Plant Hunt
rationale: Garden lesson: students orient with a map, identify summer crops and water the garden, ending with a tasting. The tasting is sampling a garden item (thumbs-up sweet/sour), not a structured sensory assessment, so no Tasting. No cooking occurs.

title: Food of Peru
rationale: Students prepare arroz chaufa (Peruvian fried rice) → Sautéing & stir-frying, with knives/cutting boards in materials and produce to cut for the dishes → Knife skills. Chicha morada and the locro components (parboiled potatoes, roasted squash) are pre-made by the teacher per prep. Graters/measuring spoons are tool-list-only with no student step named in the Agenda, so grating/measuring were not tagged; closing is eating-and-discussing, not structured tasting.

title: Foods From Around the World: China
rationale: Smashed-cucumber-salad lesson: students smash cucumbers in a bag with a rolling pin (→ Mashing), cut cucumbers (→ Knife skills), mix a dressing in a jar (→ Creating sauces & dressings), and combine into a salad (→ Assembling dishes). The hand-sanitizer note is routine handling, not a taught safety segment, so no Kitchen & food safety; the closing 'share tasting words' is a brief word share-out, not a structured sensory comparison, so no Tasting.

title: Expert's Guide to Gardening: Compost
rationale: Garden lesson: students observe the worm bin/compost and complete a workbook page. The Agenda ends 'Finish with a tasting' (eating), not a structured tasting skill. No cooking occurs.
